title: platform-user — HTTP routes
description: Nine routes, ALL on the platform_admin guard, ALL under the `/api/v1/platform/` prefix. There is NO tenant-facing surface — tenant Users cannot see platform staff. Every route runs `platform.audit` + `platform.mfa_required` middleware (auto-applied). Every route requires the caller to hold a specific `platform.platform_users.*` permission — least-privilege by design: a freshly-hired platform staff without additional roles can reach ONLY the `/platform-profiles/me` endpoints.

groups.name: platform-users-crud
groups.description: Primary CRUD surface for Stackra staff management. super_admin + hr scope for writes; ops + product + security can read.

routes.description: List. Filters: filter[department], filter[status], filter[employment_type], filter[manager_id]. Search: q (matches display_name + email fingerprint when caller has viewPii). Default sort: hire_date desc. Excludes offboarded rows unless include_offboarded=true.

Hire a new PlatformUser from an existing Identity. Body: HirePlatformUserInput. Fires PlatformUserHired on success. Refuses with 422 `IDENTITY_MFA_REQUIRED` when the target Identity has NULL mfa_secret_encrypted.
Detail view. Includes profile satellite (PII gated), manager reference, direct reports count, current on-call shift if in rotation. Redacted per PII permission gate.

Update employment metadata (department, employment_type, job_title, manager_id, hire_date). Refuses status transitions here — those go through the dedicated /suspend, /restore, /offboard endpoints so the state-machine gates run cleanly.

Temporary suspension. Body: { reason: string (min 20 chars) }. Fires PlatformUserSuspended. Revokes all active platform_admin PATs for this user + pauses (does not terminate) any active impersonation sessions.

Reverse a suspend. Body: { note?: string }. Refuses when status != 'suspended'. Fires PlatformUserRestored + PlatformUserActivated. Paused impersonation sessions do NOT auto-resume — must be explicitly re-started.

Terminal offboarding. Body: OffboardPlatformUserInput. Refuses when the target has direct reports (must be reassigned first via /reassign-manager). Fires PlatformUserOffboarded which dispatches RevokePlatformUserAccessJob to notifications-critical. Starts the 90-day retention hold clock.

90-day audit summary for this PlatformUser as actor. Query params: from, to, format (json | csv). Response: PlatformUserAuditReportData. Every invocation itself writes an audit row so 'who viewed whose trail' is auditable.

groups.name: platform-profiles-me
groups.description: Self-service surface every PlatformUser reaches to read + write their own profile. Only two abilities involved: view.self + update.self, seeded on every platform_admin baseline role.

routes.description: Read own PlatformProfile row. Always unredacted for the caller (they own the row).
Update own profile fields. Body: UpdatePlatformProfileInput. first_name + last_name are HR-controlled and rejected on self-service (caller must ask HR to update via /platform-users/{id}).

groups.name: platform-users-on-call
groups.description: On-call rotation resolver. Consumed by internal escalation tooling + the ops dashboard.

routes.description: Resolve current on-call staff via OnCallResolver. Response: PlatformUserOnCallResolutionData with unredacted phone_e164 (permission gate specifically for on-call escalation).

boundary_note: There is deliberately no /api/v1/platform-users route on the tenant plane. Tenant users cannot list, view, or interact with platform staff. The ONE legitimate bridge is the future `access/delegation` module (Wave 1b) which exposes impersonation-session start-points on both planes (a super_admin starts an impersonation from the platform plane; the tenant plane sees an audit-log entry naming the impersonator via a derived DTO that reveals only the display_name).
